title: Pit-pressure composite score
summary: Create a small number of composite pressure scores from existing target-free state features.
rationale: Many successful features are correlated components of the same strategic pressure. A bounded composite can help models capture monotonic pressure without relying on many duplicate aliases.
implementation_hint: Construct one or two additive or multiplicative scores from normalized current-tyre finish pressure, positive lap-time loss, degradation per tyre life, late-race progress, stop/debt urgency, and next-stop feasibility. Keep component features as well and avoid target fitting.
expected_effect: Small ROC AUC gain with medium confidence.
risk: Hand-built composites can encode poor assumptions; use normalized continuous inputs and keep the feature count low.
